narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after all the effort you've put into your law school dreams, and I want to walk you through why our predictive model saw your path to the bar exam as a tough one—68% chance of not passing, based on patterns from thousands of students like you. Picture your story through the model's eyes: it started with your Year 3 grades landing you in the bottom decile of your school, the single biggest red flag that echoed struggles seen in others who couldn't rally for the bar. That early dip in Year 1, also bottom decile, built on it, painting a picture of consistent academic hurdles right from the start. Your LSAT score of 34 didn't help lift that weight, as it sat lower than what typically pulls students through the grind. Coming from a middle-income family added another layer, since the model links that bracket to fewer resources for the intense prep many need. Your undergrad GPA of 2.9 reinforced the trend, suggesting the rigorous law school pace might stay challenging. Even as a white female going full-time, those details nudged the outlook downward in ways the model has learned from past outcomes—nothing personal, just data patterns. It's not that your target law passion or determination aren't there; it's that this combination has tripped up so many before. The good news? You're not defined by a prediction—you can target those weak spots, like boosting study strategies or seeking extra support, to rewrite your story. Plenty of students have turned similar profiles around with focused effort. Hang in there; this is just one chapter, and you've got the drive to make the next one a success.
